# go-queue

> `go-queue` is a consolidated Go worker queue with owned implementations for in-memory, Redis Pub/Sub, Redis Streams, NATS, NSQ, and RabbitMQ. It began as a compatibility-focused merge of the `golang-queue` ecosystem and intentionally keeps that programming model recognizable while fixing correctness and operability gaps in one release unit.

This index is generated from the repository's canonical Markdown documentation.

## Documentation

- [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/architecture.md): The module has a small coordinator at its root and explicit backend packages. It deliberately avoids a universal transport API that would erase correctness differences.
- [Lifecycle and state ownership](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/lifecycle.md): `Queue` owns scheduling and handler execution; each backend worker owns its transport connection, delivery channel, and settlement callbacks. A queue moves through `constructed -> started -> shutting down -> stopped` and never returns to an earlier state.
- [Delivery semantics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/delivery-semantics.md): No backend in this module promises exactly-once processing. Handlers must be idempotent whenever a transport can redeliver.
- [Threat and failure model](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/failure-model.md): The trust boundary begins at every enqueue caller and broker delivery. Broker availability, network continuity, handler correctness, and observer code are not assumed.
- [Security and abuse resistance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/security.md): Every enqueue caller and broker delivery is untrusted. The library validates message envelopes before handler execution, but deployment authentication, authorization, network isolation, and broker retention limits remain operator responsibilities.
- [Hardening report](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/hardening-report.md): This report records the audit findings, their evidence, impact, and current disposition. Severity reflects production worker impact, not patch size.
- [Integration evidence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/integration-evidence.md): Integration tests use the `integration` build tag and Testcontainers 0.42.0. Images are pinned by version and digest so a tag cannot silently change the evidence environment.
- [Performance and capacity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/performance.md): Benchmarks are diagnostic measurements, not correctness gates. `make benchmark` uses a bounded iteration count (`BENCH_TIME=100x`) so broker-backed harnesses do not amplify load unpredictably.
- [Public API reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/api.md): Go package documentation remains authoritative for exact signatures. This page maps the stable concepts new adopters need.
- [Adoption guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/adoption.md): 1. Choose semantics before choosing a client. Use Redis Streams for durable Redis-first work and Pub/Sub only for disposable notifications. 2. Make the handler idempotent. At-least-once transports can repeat completed side effects after a crash/ack race. 3. Construct with `NewWorkerE` and fail service startup on error. 4. Set explicit worker count, task timeout, and retry limits. 5. Export `Metric` counters and `Observer` failure/retry/shutdown events. 6. Exercise broker loss and shutdown with production-like timeouts. 7. Roll out with a small consumer group and compare throughput, age, retries, and duplicates before full migration.
- [Migration from golang-queue](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/migration.md): | Upstream | Consolidated import | | --- | --- | | `github.com/golang-queue/queue` | `github.com/faustbrian/go-queue` | | `github.com/golang-queue/redisdb` | `github.com/faustbrian/go-queue/redisdb` | | `github.com/golang-queue/redisdb-stream` | `github.com/faustbrian/go-queue/redisstream` | | `github.com/golang-queue/nats` | `github.com/faustbrian/go-queue/nats` | | `github.com/golang-queue/nsq` | `github.com/faustbrian/go-queue/nsq` | | `github.com/golang-queue/rabbitmq` | `github.com/faustbrian/go-queue/rabbitmq` |
- [Compatibility policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/compatibility.md): The project uses semantic versioning.
- [Scenario cookbook](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/cookbook.md): Set `RetryCount` and `RetryDelay` on `job.AllowOption`. The total timeout covers handler execution and retry waits.
- [FAQ](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/faq.md): No. Durable backends target at-least-once processing. Applications must handle duplicates.
- [Troubleshooting](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/troubleshooting.md): Check the backend address format, credentials, TLS mode, and broker listener. Use `NewWorkerE`; do not recover a compatibility-constructor panic and continue.
- [Versioning and release guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/docs/releases.md): Releases follow semantic versioning and are created from annotated `vX.Y.Z` tags on a clean `main` commit synchronized with `origin/main`.
- [Roadmap](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/ROADMAP.md): tagged containers.
- [Changelog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/CHANGELOG.md): All notable changes are documented here. The project follows semantic versioning and Keep a Changelog structure.

## Complete documentation

- [llms-full.txt](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/faustbrian/go-queue/main/llms-full.txt): All linked documentation in one file.
